report.title: Ethereum multisig wallet
report.commonImplementation: An N-of-M multisig smart contract (e.g. a Safe) where owners submit and confirm transactions on-chain.

trustComparison.source: the contract bytecode and an RPC node to report state
trustComparison.infrix: no node — the proof is re-checked from its own bytes

trustComparison.source: N-of-M owner keys on a single contract
trustComparison.infrix: governed key pages with policy-gated approval and signed decisions

uxComparison.infrix: run a scenario; local runs need no deploy and no funds

uxComparison.source: each owner sends an on-chain confirmation tx
uxComparison.infrix: each approver records a signed, artifact-bound decision

proofComparison.source: the transaction's on-chain receipt (you trust the chain/RPC)
proofComparison.infrix: A portable proof story re-verified from its own bytes — the node is never trusted.

proofComparison.source: requires trusting an RPC/explorer or re-deriving from chain state
proofComparison.infrix: anyone can run `infrix verify` offline and reproduce the verdict
L3 locally; L4 with a live Accumulate L0 anchor confirmation (never claimed without it)

costComparison.label: Cost & latency
costComparison.source: per-operation execution cost on the source chain (varies with network conditions)
costComparison.infrix: metered in internal units (IU) derived from the governed verdict; local runs need no network

sourceRef.citation: Qualitative only; no measured numbers. Supply your own benchmark to quantify.

costComparison.disclaimer: Qualitative only — no measured numbers are asserted; supply a benchmark to quantify either side.

migrationPlan.title: Map owners to key pages
migrationPlan.detail: Each multisig owner becomes a governed identity / key page.

migrationPlan.title: Express the N-of-M rule as policy
migrationPlan.detail: The release policy requires the approvals (e.g. buyer + seller).

migrationPlan.title: Run and prove
migrationPlan.detail: Run the governed escrow; collect signed approvals; export a portable proof.

claims.text: An Infrix proof can be re-verified offline from its own bytes, without trusting the producing node.
A governed run produces a portable proof story; locally it reaches L3, and a live Accumulate L0 anchor on Kermit raises it to L4.
Approvals are recorded as signed decisions bound to the exact artifact hash.
A Safe-style multisig confirms a transaction once N of M owners submit on-chain confirmations.

source.citation: Ethereum smart-contract multisig pattern (e.g. Gnosis Safe) — public contract docs

report.proofOutput: A portable proof story showing the approvals and release happened under policy, re-verifiable offline.
whatRemainsHard: key custody is still the user's responsibility
the people behind the approving identities must be trustworthy
infrixTrustModel: The producing node is never trusted: a proof is re-verified from its own bytes.
Authority is governed key pages with policy-gated, signed approvals.
A live Accumulate L0 anchor confirmation raises a local L3 proof to L4 (never claimed without it).
Selective disclosure reveals only the fields a policy authorizes; the rest stays encrypted.
Independent witnesses can attest an outcome, so no single node is an authority on it.

Ethereum smart-contract multisig pattern.citation: Ethereum smart-contract multisig pattern (e.g. Gnosis Safe) — public contract docs
